title: Z.AI completes a roughly 1-gigawatt data center built entirely on Chinese-made AI chips
dedup_check: clean (no archive match), cycle 3 -- distinct from all other Zhipu/Z.AI-adjacent items in the archive (none existed prior)
watch: Identity of the domestic chip vendor used in the facility, if it becomes citable
Independent confirmation (or denial) directly from Z.AI/Zhipu itself
Whether the cluster is used to train and demonstrably ships a frontier-scale model (capacity vs. capability)
The Zhongke Jiahe/XCore Sigma acquisition, if a second qualifying Tier<=2 source becomes available
cycle_2026-07-23_night_c3_note: Carried forward from cycle 2's report as the strongest of several flagged Tier-0/press leads ('clears the independence bar easily'). Fresh verifier sub-agent (17 tool calls) found the core claim -- 1GW datacenter, all-domestic chips -- independently confirmed by Bloomberg's exclusive and SCMP's separately-sourced report (SCMP does not cite Bloomberg), satisfying the wire-exclusive independence check; flagged several supporting specifics (chip vendor identity, cluster chip count, GLM-5.2 training-provenance claim, exact stock-move percentage) as single-source or unconfirmed and recommended excluding them. Fresh adversarial falsifier (11 tool calls) found no denials/retractions, broad corroboration across English and Chinese-language press, confirmed the story is not stale/recycled, and independently re-fetched SCMP to validate its sourcing is genuinely separate from Bloomberg. Published claim set deliberately narrowed to the two claims that cleared the bar cleanly; chip vendor, cluster-size, acquisition, and stock-move details all held back per verifier/falsifier recommendation rather than published on thin sourcing. Style gates: EN pass, TR pass.
